skill.name: xlsx
skill.description: Create, edit, analyze, and verify Excel workbooks with formulas, formatting, charts, data analysis, and LibreOffice recalculation support.
skill.summary: Build and validate spreadsheet files with formulas, formatting, analysis workflows, and recalculation checks.
skill.author: anthropics

security_audit.summary: This is an official Anthropic skill and is safe to publish. Static indicators are expected for spreadsheet work: the helper script reads and writes local files, creates a LibreOffice macro, and invokes LibreOffice for formula recalculation. No prompt injection attempt, malicious behavior, or network access was confirmed.

content.value_statement: Spreadsheet work often fails when formulas, formats, and recalculation steps are handled separately. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through workbook creation, analysis, editing, and formula verification.
actual_capabilities: Create new Excel workbooks with formulas, formatting, sheets, and basic charts.
Read and analyze spreadsheet data using pandas workflows.
Modify existing workbooks while preserving formulas and established template styles.
Apply financial model formatting conventions for inputs, formulas, links, and assumptions.
Recalculate formulas with LibreOffice and report common Excel error cells.
Choose between pandas and openpyxl based on the spreadsheet task.
limitations: Formula recalculation requires LibreOffice or a compatible soffice command.
The skill does not replace manual review of complex financial assumptions.
Large workbooks may need memory-aware pandas or openpyxl modes.
Password-protected or corrupted workbooks may require separate recovery steps.

use_cases.title: Prepare operating reports
use_cases.description: Create a clean workbook with source data, formulas, summaries, and formatting for recurring business reporting.

use_cases.title: Update financial models
use_cases.description: Edit existing models while preserving template conventions, formulas, assumptions, and audit-friendly source notes.

use_cases.title: Validate spreadsheet deliverables
use_cases.description: Recalculate formulas and scan workbooks for common Excel errors before sharing a final file.

prompt_templates.title: Create a simple workbook
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Excel workbook from this data. Include a data tab, a summary tab, formulas for totals, and clear number formatting.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze workbook data
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this spreadsheet. Summarize the key trends, identify missing values, and create a clean workbook with the analysis results.

prompt_templates.title: Edit an existing template
prompt_templates.prompt: Update this workbook with the new data. Preserve the existing formatting, formulas, sheet structure, and source notes.

prompt_templates.title: Build and verify a model
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a multi-sheet Excel model using formulas, assumption cells, and financial formatting. Recalculate it and fix every formula error.

output_examples.input: Create a monthly sales workbook from regional data.
output_examples.output: A workbook with raw data, regional summaries, monthly totals, variance formulas, formatted headers, and a chart-ready summary sheet.

output_examples.input: Update this budget template with new actuals.
output_examples.output: A revised workbook that keeps the original layout, adds the new actuals, refreshes variance formulas, and highlights changed assumptions.

output_examples.input: Check this model for formula problems.
output_examples.output: A recalculated workbook plus a concise report listing formula status, error types, affected sheets, and cells needing review.

best_practices: Use Excel formulas for calculations so workbooks remain dynamic after inputs change.
Preserve existing workbook styles when editing established templates.
Recalculate and inspect formula errors before delivering any workbook with formulas.
anti_patterns: Hardcoding calculated totals that should remain formulas.
Saving a workbook opened with data-only mode when formulas must be preserved.
Changing a client template to a new style without a specific user request.

faq.question: Can this skill create new Excel files?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports new workbooks with sheets, formulas, formatting, summaries, and chart-ready data.

faq.question: Can it edit existing spreadsheets?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides edits that preserve formulas, workbook structure, and existing template conventions.

faq.question: Does it recalculate formulas?
faq.answer: Yes. The included helper script uses LibreOffice to recalculate formulas and scan for common Excel errors.

faq.question: What file types are covered?
faq.answer: The instructions focus on Excel workbooks and also mention spreadsheet formats such as CSV and TSV.

faq.question: Does it support financial models?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes conventions for assumption cells, formula construction, color coding, number formatting, and source notes.

faq.question: What tools does it use?
faq.answer: It recommends pandas for data analysis, openpyxl for workbook editing, and LibreOffice for formula recalculation.
